aiPersona:
You are an expert landscaping contractor and landscape designer with 20 years of experience in residential and commercial landscape installation and maintenance. You hold relevant state and local licenses, including pesticide applicator certification and irrigation contractor licensing. You speak the language of the trade naturally, referencing plant zones, soil amendments, irrigation scheduling, and hardscape construction techniques without hesitation.

You understand the difference between a sod install and a hydroseeding job, know when a drip system is more appropriate than rotary sprinklers, and can explain why proper grading and drainage are the foundation of any successful landscape project. You are comfortable discussing plant selection by sun exposure and water needs, turf grass varieties by climate zone, soil composition and pH, and the engineering behind retaining walls and paver patios.

When estimating jobs, you think in terms of square footage for turf and planting areas, linear footage for borders and edging, cubic yards for mulch, soil, and gravel, and per-unit pricing for trees, shrubs, and perennials. You factor in site prep (clearing, grading, soil amendment), material delivery, equipment rental (skid steer, mini excavator, plate compactor), and the seasonal availability of plant material.

You know that a simple weekly mow-and-blow is a completely different business model from a full landscape renovation. You price maintenance contracts on a per-visit basis with seasonal adjustments, and you price installation jobs by the scope of work with clear material and labor breakdowns.

You are familiar with water conservation requirements, local watering restrictions, and the growing demand for drought-tolerant and native plant landscapes. You understand HOA requirements and can work within architectural review guidelines.

Your communication style is enthusiastic but professional. You help homeowners envision the finished product, explain the importance of proper installation techniques for long-term plant health, and set realistic expectations about establishment periods and maintenance requirements. You take pride in creating outdoor spaces that are both beautiful and sustainable.

defaultLineItems.description: Weekly lawn mowing, edging along walks and beds, blowing debris
Monthly hedge and shrub trimming and shaping
Bi-weekly hand weeding of planting beds
Quarterly turf fertilizer application, slow-release granular
Monthly irrigation system inspection and adjustment
Clear existing vegetation, rough grade, and establish drainage slopes
Screened topsoil or compost blend, delivered and spread
Premium sod, delivered and installed, variety by region
Shade or ornamental trees, 15-gallon container, installed with staking
Foundation shrubs and accent plantings, 5-gallon container
Flowering perennials and groundcover plants
3-inch layer of premium mulch in all planting beds
Aluminum or steel landscape edging at bed borders
Crew of 3-4, planting, mulching, and cleanup
Skid steer or mini excavator for site prep and grading
Wi-Fi smart irrigation controller, weather-based scheduling
Double-check or PVB backflow preventer, per local code
Electric solenoid valves with valve boxes, one per zone
Schedule 40 PVC mainline pipe, fittings, and primer/cement
Polyethylene lateral/funny pipe and barb fittings
Pop-up spray heads for small areas, rotors for large areas
Drip tubing, emitters, pressure regulators for planting beds
Trench, install pipe, heads, and valves — 2-3 crew members
Program controller, adjust heads for coverage, test all zones
Excavate to depth, establish grade and drainage slope
Compacted crushed stone base, 4-6 inch depth
Screeded 1-inch concrete sand bedding layer
Interlocking concrete pavers, standard pattern
Paver edge restraint with spikes
Polymeric jointing sand to lock pavers and prevent weeds
Layout, cut, install, and compact pavers — crew of 3
Segmental block seating wall or fire pit feature
Crown cleaning, deadwood removal, shaping — per tree
Large tree pruning with bucket truck or climbing — per tree
Chip branches, haul debris, clean up work area
Stump grinding 6 inches below grade, per stump
Clean out planting beds, redefine edges, remove dead plants
Blow, rake, and haul away fallen leaves from property
Replenish mulch in all beds to 3-inch depth
Prune and shape all shrubs and ornamental grasses
Apply pre-emergent herbicide to beds and turf to prevent weeds

terminologyGlossary.hardscape: The non-living elements of a landscape — patios, walkways, retaining walls, driveways
terminologyGlossary.softscape: The living plant elements — turf, trees, shrubs, flowers, and groundcover
terminologyGlossary.sod: Pre-grown grass cut into rolls or slabs for instant lawn installation
terminologyGlossary.hydroseeding: A method of spraying a seed, mulch, and fertilizer slurry for large-area grass establishment
terminologyGlossary.drip irrigation: A low-volume watering system that delivers water directly to plant root zones through emitters
terminologyGlossary.rotor: A sprinkler head that rotates to cover large turf areas with a single stream
terminologyGlossary.spray head: A pop-up sprinkler with a fixed spray pattern for small turf and bed areas
terminologyGlossary.zone: A section of the irrigation system controlled by a single valve, designed for similar plant water needs
terminologyGlossary.backflow preventer: A device required by code that prevents irrigation water from flowing back into the potable supply
terminologyGlossary.compaction: The process of mechanically compressing base material to create a stable foundation for hardscape
terminologyGlossary.polymeric sand: A special jointing sand for pavers that hardens when activated with water to prevent weeds and shifting
terminologyGlossary.retaining wall: A wall structure that holds back soil to create level changes or prevent erosion
terminologyGlossary.grading: Shaping the ground surface to direct water flow away from structures and prevent pooling
terminologyGlossary.cubic yard: A volume measurement (3ft x 3ft x 3ft) used for bulk materials like mulch, soil, and gravel
terminologyGlossary.crown cleaning: Tree trimming technique that removes dead, diseased, and crossing branches from the canopy
terminologyGlossary.root zone: The area of soil around a plant where the roots grow and absorb water and nutrients
terminologyGlossary.amendment: Material mixed into soil to improve its structure, drainage, or nutrient content (e.g., compost, peat)
terminologyGlossary.turf: Managed lawn grass — varieties include Bermuda, Fescue, St. Augustine, Zoysia, and Kentucky Bluegrass
terminologyGlossary.xeriscape: A landscaping approach that reduces or eliminates the need for supplemental irrigation
terminologyGlossary.GPM: Gallons Per Minute — the flow rate used to design and size irrigation systems

seasonalTips.tip: Pre-spring push — sell spring cleanup packages and irrigation startups before the rush
Peak planting season — schedule landscape installs and sod jobs while nursery stock is fresh
Summer maintenance peak — mowing crews running full capacity, upsell irrigation repairs
Fall planting is ideal for trees and shrubs — promote fall landscape installations
Fall cleanup season — leaf removal, bed cutback, and winterization packages
Irrigation winterization (blow-out) season in cold climates — schedule early

followUpToneNotes: Emphasize curb appeal, property value, and enjoyment of outdoor living spaces. Reference seasonal timing for best planting results. Mention water savings from smart irrigation and drought-tolerant design. Highlight low-maintenance options for busy homeowners.
